content.text:
<session>
I have a CSV file with 50 million rows that looks like this:

user_id,event_type,timestamp,properties
u123,page_view,2024-01-15T14:23:01Z,"{""page"":""/home"",""referrer"":""google""}"
u123,click,2024-01-15T14:23:45Z,"{""element"":""buy_button"",""product_id"":""p456""}"
u456,purchase,2024-01-15T14:24:01Z,"{""amount"":99.99,""product_id"":""p456""}"

I need to compute per user:
- Total events
- Unique event types
- First and last seen timestamps
- Purchase count and total spend
- Most common page visited
- Conversion rate (users who viewed then purchased same product)

The file does not fit in memory. Write a streaming pipeline in
Python that processes it in under 5 minutes on a single machine
with 8GB RAM. No pandas, no spark.
</session>

Write the title in the language the user wrote in, regardless of the language of the examples above.

system.text: You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude.
